title: centaurea cineraria subsp. circae
intro: Centaurea cineraria subsp. circae, commonly known as silver-gray cineraria, is a type of flowering plant in the daisy family, characterized by its silvery-gray leaves and small, blue flowers. This plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Centaurea cineraria subsp. circae include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in teas, tinctures, and salves, making it a popular choice for natural remedies and skincare products.
